name: Baena from Cabra — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite
name_original: Baena desde Cabra — circular por la Vía Verde del Aceite
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cabra traverse a landscape characterized by the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, extensive olive groves, and Mediterranean woodland. The region features the Vía Verde del Aceite, a disused railway line repurposed for cycling, offering accessible paths through varied terrain. Karst springs and unique geological formations are also present, providing diverse natural features for exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Cabra The most popular touring cycling route is Baena from Cabra — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the historic Vía Verde del Aceite, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alamedal Viaduct – Beautiful section of the greenway loop from Cabra, a moderate 40.9 miles (65.8 km) path. This route showcases preserved railway infrastructure, including the Alamedal Viaduct, within the greenway. Local touring cyclists also love the Venta Los Pelaos loop from Cabra, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail leading through olive groves and rolling hills, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Cabra is defined by the Vía Verde del Aceite, the rugged terrain of the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park, and vast olive groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Cabra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cabra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cabra?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes around Cabra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 70 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cabra?
A: Touring cycling around Cabra offers diverse terrain. You'll find accessible, often paved paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/strong&gt;, which is built on a disused railway line. For more challenging rides, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; provides dramatic scenery with significant elevation gains, rugged beauty, and panoramic views. The region is also characterized by vast olive groves and Mediterranean woodland.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Cabra?
A: Yes, Cabra offers several easier routes, with 5 routes specifically categorized as easy. The &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/strong&gt; is particularly well-suited for less experienced cyclists or those seeking a more relaxed ride, as it features gentle slopes and well-maintained surfaces. Many sections are suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Cabra?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Cabra are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025890341/baena-from-cabra-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baena from Cabra — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamedal Viaduct – Beautiful section of the greenway loop from Cabra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cabra's touring cycling routes are rich with landmarks. Along the Vía Verde del Aceite, you can experience preserved 19th-century railway infrastructure, including impressive viaducts like the Alamedal Viaduct and the historic Plantío tunnel. The region also features the beautiful &lt;strong&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/strong&gt; natural park with its karst spring and waterfalls. For those interested in historical sites, the town's castle and the Church of the Assumption and Angels can be incorporated into tours. You can also explore nearby natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1045186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Limestone formations and caves in the Bailón Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Cabra a good destination for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: Yes, Cabra is suitable for family-friendly touring cycling, especially along the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/strong&gt;. This greenway offers accessible, often paved paths with gentle gradients, making it ideal for families with children or those looking for a relaxed ride. There are also rest areas with benches and picnic tables along the route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cabra?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Cabra are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winter can be cooler but still offers opportunities for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there services for cyclists along the Vía Verde del Aceite?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Aceite is well-equipped for cyclists. You'll find rest areas with benches, picnic tables, and bike racks. There are also bike rental services available at locations like the Subbética Bike Centre for Tourists at the Doña Mencía station. Some old railway stations along the route have been repurposed as restaurants or cyclotourist centers, and interpretation centers provide information about the greenway and the surrounding natural park.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, several old railway stations along the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/strong&gt; have been converted into restaurants or cyclotourist centers, offering opportunities for refreshments and meals. Additionally, the towns and villages surrounding Cabra, such as Doña Mencía, provide various options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation, allowing you to plan your route with convenient stops.

Q: What makes the touring cycling experience in Cabra unique?
A: The touring cycling experience in Cabra is unique due to its combination of the accessible &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/strong&gt;, which showcases historic railway architecture and winds through vast olive groves, and the more challenging, dramatic landscapes of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. This blend offers routes for all abilities, from gentle rides through agricultural scenery to demanding climbs with breathtaking panoramic views, all within a region rich in natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that feature unique geological formations or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which Cabra is a gateway to, is known for its unique geological formations and dramatic scenery. Routes within the park often lead to stunning viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Fuente del Río&lt;/strong&gt; natural park also features a beautiful karst spring. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that pass near highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6073437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Lomas&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de la Tiñosa&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
This is another of the magnificent metal viaducts that we will cross on our tour of the Vía Verde del Aceite.
It extends over 132 meters and reaches a height of 50 meters, giving us beautiful views over the olive grove.

The surface is usually in very good condition, but there is no lighting. If you arrive in the sun, you don't need much, but if you arrive in the afternoon or at night, I recommend that you have a flashlight handy.

The Doña Mencía station is the only one of the stations that we will find along the nature trail, built on one floor.
A few meters from the passenger building and on the same side of the road, there is a building that has been rehabilitated as a bicycle center. This is the old freight dock of the station. The goods were located on open-air docks or other covered docks, depending on their economic value or characteristics. The closed docks consisted of open roof naves, with a small office for the clerk who was responsible for the delivery notes. On one side, the wagons were loaded or unloaded, while on the other side, the vehicles that arrived by road did so. In order to protect the goods during loading and unloading, these buildings were designed with a gable roof that ends in two large lateral overhangs.

This village is one of the most picturesque in the province of Cordoba. Its location against the slope is magnificent. It is dominated by a stately castle carved into a rock above the abyss. Its charm is enhanced by the whitewashed houses on narrow, steep streets and squares full of flowers.
Tempting stop on the route if you are doing the greenway: the old Luque station now houses a bar that invites you to stop and recharge your batteries.

The town of Baena lives on olives. Its entire economy is largely based on the exploitation of the olive tree and its juicy elixir. There is a very interesting olive museum to visit.
The "Museo del olivar y el Aceite" opened in 2003. It is installed in an old mill. On two floors, we discuss the history and different uses of the olive, and even its stone which serves as energy fuel. Note that in November, the museum organizes olive and oil days with exhibitions, a series of conferences, competitions and gastronomic tours.

waypoints.name: Restaurant and Olive Oil Interpretation Center in the Old Luque Station
